Generate a customized datatable

Source: R/helpers-rmd.R
generate_datatable.Rd

This function creates a DT::datatable object with default settings for horizontal scrolling (scrollX = TRUE) and no table controls (dom = "t"). It supports input data of types data.table or DFrame. Additional options and arguments can be passed to customize the table further.

Usage

generate_datatable(
  tab,
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, dom = "ftip"),
  width = "100%",
  col_to_round = NULL,
  digits = 3,
  ...
)

Arguments

tab

A dataset to be displayed in the datatable. Must be of class data.table or DFrame.

options

A list of options to customize the DataTable. Defaults to list(scrollX = TRUE, dom = "ftip") - to display the search box, the table itself, information about the number of rows and pagination

width

A character string specifying the width of the table. Defaults to "100%".

col_to_round

A character vector with the names of the columns whose values should be rounded.

digits

A numeric value indicating the number of decimal places to be used for rounding

...

Additional arguments passed to DT::datatable.

Value

A DT::datatable object.

Author

Bartosz Czech czech.bartosz@external.gene.com

Examples

generate_datatable(data.table::data.table(iris))

On this page
